senses.definition: a plan or timetable outlining the sequence of events or activities

examples.x: She checked her schedule to see when her next meeting was.
The project manager created a detailed schedule to ensure all tasks were completed on time.
He adjusted his schedule to accommodate the unexpected changes in his travel plans.
Students received their class schedules for the upcoming semester.
The construction company adhered to a strict schedule to finish the project ahead of the deadline.

senses.definition: each part into which a day is divided at a school, university, etc.

examples.x: During the first period, the class focused on mathematics, tackling complex equations and problem-solving.
The teacher announced a surprise quiz during the third period, catching the students off guard.
In the afternoon period, the school offers elective courses such as art and music for students to explore their interests.

senses.definition: the time when students are at school, attending classes and activities, usually from morning to afternoon

examples.x: The school day starts at 8 AM and ends at 3 PM.
Teachers often plan their lessons according to the school day’s schedule.
During the school day, students attend different classes, such as math, science, and history.
Some students stay after the school day ends for extracurricular activities.
After a long school day, I’m always ready to relax at home.

senses.definition: each of the two periods into which a year at schools or universities is divided

examples.x: At the end of each semester, students receive a report card with their grades.
During the semester break, many students travel to visit their families.
In the first semester, we covered basic grammar and vocabulary in the language course.
My favorite time of the semester is when we have group discussions and debates in our literature class.
This semester, I am taking classes in English, math, and history.

senses.definition: a scheduled period of teaching, instruction, or learning activities conducted within a defined timeframe

examples.x: The morning session consisted of three classes, including mathematics, science, and English.
During the session, the teacher introduced a new topic and guided students through interactive learning exercises.
Students actively participated in discussions and group activities throughout the session.
The session concluded with a brief recap of the day's lesson and an assignment for homework.
The afternoon session began with a hands-on laboratory experiment to reinforce concepts learned earlier in the day.

senses.definition: one of the three periods in the academic year during which multiple classes are held in schools, universities, etc.

examples.x: The new term starts next Monday.
She completed her assignments before the term ended.
He enrolled in three courses for the spring term.
The term lasts for three months.
She earned good grades in the previous term.

senses.definition: the period during which regular academic sessions or terms are conducted in schools or educational institutions
a break from formal education or employment, usually lasting a year, to pursue personal interests, travel, or other experiences

examples.x: After graduating from high school, Sarah decided to take a gap year to travel around Europe before starting college.
John took a gap year to work on a farm in Australia and gain hands-on experience in agriculture.
The university encourages students to consider taking a gap year to explore different cultures and broaden their horizons before entering the workforce.
Maria spent her gap year volunteering at an orphanage in Africa, where she gained valuable life skills and made a positive impact on the community.
Mark used his gap year to learn a new language and study abroad in Japan, immersing himself in the culture and language.

senses.definition: the period of the year during which schools and universities hold classes

examples.x: The academic year at the university starts in September and ends in June.
The students are preparing for the exams at the end of the academic year.
The academic year in some countries begins in August and ends in May.
The calendar for the academic year includes holidays and mid-term breaks.
Many schools have a break between terms during the academic year.

senses.definition: a specific time during the school day when students have the opportunity to work on homework or study independently under supervision

examples.x: Students used the study hall period to review notes and prepare for the upcoming exam.
The study hall session provided dedicated time for students to complete their assignments in a quiet environment.
During study hall, students could seek clarification from teachers on difficult concepts or problems.
The school implemented study hall periods to promote academic accountability and foster good study habits.
Some students utilized the study hall time to collaborate on group projects or study together with peers.

senses.definition: a duration of time at school when children are free to exit their classroom and spend their time playing outside
a scheduled break between lessons or classes in a school; allowing students to engage in relaxing activities

examples.x: The students eagerly awaited recess to play outside.
Recess is the students' favorite time to relax and recharge.
She brought her lunch to eat during recess with her friends.
The teacher announced that recess would start early today.
During recess, the playground was filled with laughter and games.

senses.definition: a span of time which we do not work or go to school, and spend traveling or resting instead, particularly in a different city, country, etc.

examples.x: We went on a vacation to Hawaii last summer.
My family is planning a vacation to Europe next month.
After working hard for months, I finally took a vacation to the mountains.
They are going on a vacation to the beach for two weeks.
I need a vacation to relax and recharge my batteries.

senses.definition: a part of a school day in which there is no class

examples.x: I have a free period after lunch, so I usually use that time to catch up on homework.
We decided to study together in the cafeteria during our free period.
She spent her free period in the library, reading for her next class.
He finished his project during a free period, which gave him more time for other tasks.
I don't have any plans for my free period today, so I might just relax.

senses.definition: a period of time during the school day when students are not in class, often used for relaxation, refreshment, or other activities
an educational option provided by universities and colleges for people who are not able to study full time
a break in the school calendar, typically lasting for one week, occurring midway through a term or semester

examples.x: Families often plan vacations during the half-term break to spend quality time together.
The school announced various activities and workshops for students to participate in during half-term.
Teachers assigned minimal homework over the half-term break to allow students to recharge and relax.
The half-term holiday provided students with an opportunity to catch up on sleep and unwind from the demands of school.
Many students looked forward to the half-term break as a chance to explore new hobbies or interests outside of school.

senses.definition: a paid leave from work, often taken every seven years, for study or personal growth

examples.x: She took a sabbatical to write her novel.
During his sabbatical, he traveled to study ancient cultures.
On her sabbatical, she focused on completing her book.

senses.definition: a formal permission to be absent, especially from a school or other institution

examples.x: The boarding school granted him an exeat, allowing him to leave campus for the weekend to visit his family.
Students must obtain permission from the school administration before taking an exeat to leave campus during term time.
The parents signed their child's exeat form, indicating approval for them to attend a special event off campus.
Exeats are typically granted for specific purposes, such as medical appointments or family emergencies.
Upon returning from their exeat, students are required to sign back in at the school's reception desk.
